acceptance_criteria: Agent profiles are defined in agent-runtime-config with: role, system_prompt, model, mcp_servers, skills, tool_access_policy, and per-project overrides.
When the daemon spawns an agent, it automatically configures the agent's MCP connections based on its profile — the agent receives only the tools its role permits.
The built-in ao CLI MCP server is available to all agents by default, with tool access scoped by the agent's tool_access_policy (e.g., EM gets task.* and queue.*, PO gets requirement.* and vision.*).
Custom MCP servers can be registered per-project in .ao/config.json with command/args/env definitions, and assigned to agent profiles.
At least 3 built-in agent personas exist: EM (Engineering Manager), PO (Product Owner), SWE (Software Engineer), each with distinct system prompts, tool access, and default behaviors.
Agent profiles support a skills array that defines reusable prompt fragments or behavioral directives injected into the agent's context.
Tool access policies support both allowlist (only these tools) and blocklist (everything except these) patterns with glob-style matching (e.g., 'ao.task.*').
Project-level overrides in .ao/config.json can extend or restrict any agent profile field without modifying the global config.
The MCP server injection works for all supported CLI tools (codex, claude, gemini, opencode) using existing native MCP config mechanisms.
Agent profiles are manageable via CLI commands: ao agent-profile list, ao agent-profile show <id>, ao agent-profile create, ao agent-profile update.
Inter-agent communication is possible through a shared MCP tool (ao.agent.message) that posts messages to other running agents' context.
title: Agent persona system with role-based MCP tool access, custom servers, skills, and inter-agent communication
